title: Create User - Happy Path with Valid Mandatory Details
description: Verify that an admin can create a new user by providing all required fields with valid data. The system should return HTTP 201, generate a unique userId, set status to ACTIVE, and populate audit fields.
preconditions: Admin user has a valid JWT token with CREATE_USER permission.
No existing user with email john.doe@test.com in the system.

steps.action: Send POST request to /api/v1/users with required headers and payload.
steps.expected_result: Response status 201 Created and response body contains userId, message, status='ACTIVE', createdDate in ISO8601 format.

steps.action: Validate that the response payload includes a non‑empty userId following the pattern USR[0-9]+.
Verify that the audit fields createdBy equals the admin's userId and createdDate is a recent timestamp.
steps.expected_result: createdBy equals admin identifier; createdDate is within 5 seconds of request time.

expected_result: User is created successfully, HTTP 201 returned, all response fields populated correctly, and audit information recorded.

title: Create User - Duplicate Email Failure
description: Ensure that the system rejects creation of a user when the email address already exists, returning the appropriate error code and message.
preconditions: A user with email duplicate.user@test.com already exists in the system.

steps.action: Send POST request to /api/v1/users with payload containing the duplicate email.
steps.expected_result: Response status 409 Conflict (or defined error status) with errorCode USR_409 and message 'Email already exists'.

expected_result: User creation is blocked, appropriate error response returned, no new user record is created.
